pi_items.source: export_result.sh script confirms this path; setup_task.sh creates /home/ga/GIS_Data/exports directory
setup_task.sh confirms data location; file contains 3 points in WGS84/EPSG:4326 (coordinates: [-122.4, 37.6], [-122.3, 37.7], [-122.1, 37.65])
QGIS documentation and multi-ring buffer algorithm: 3 input points × 3 rings = 9 output features. Each ring is a separate feature in the output.
Task description explicitly specifies 'Number of rings: 3' as a parameter for the multi-ring buffer algorithm
Task description explicitly specifies 'Distance between rings: 500 (meters)' after reprojection to EPSG:32610 (UTM Zone 10N)

QGIS documentation for multi-ring buffer (constant distance) confirms output is polygon vector layer. Web search: 'Buffer zones are always vector polygons. This applies to multi-ring buffers as well.'

privileged_info_summary: The task uses synthetic GeoJSON data with 3 points in the San Francisco Bay Area (WGS84 coordinates: -122.4 to -122.1 longitude, 37.6 to 37.7 latitude). The multi-ring buffer algorithm with 3 rings and 500-meter spacing produces 9 polygon features (3 points × 3 rings). EPSG:32610 (UTM Zone 10N) is the correct projected coordinate system for distance calculations in this region. Output includes polygon geometries with ringId and distance attributes.
